Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. Our contractors Avidety may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. During this time, you were connected to a generator to provide you with electricity whilst we carry out the works. To disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily. This work is taking place in and around North Elham in Folkstone and Hythe. A road closure will be in place to allow the work to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

Steps.Message:
Hi, I'm Ebony and I'm the manager of the Messaging Team here at UK Power Networks. 

You may notice that we have less of our teams, vehicles and partners in the Colworth Road area after today. I wanted to offer you my personal reassurance that even though we have less physical presence on site, our partners, Haste, will still be working to visit each property to repair or replace damaged equipment.

Should you need to speak to Haste, they can be contacted on 01730 897100. As this will be the last message from my team, I wanted to extend a final and sincere apology for the inconvenience caused by what has been a very uncommon and complex fault with our network. Thank you.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Good evening. Just a final update to advise that our Customer Champions, the food van and Customer Support vehicle have all now left site.

They will be returning to the area from 9am tomorrow morning and we will let you know. If you have any further questions or concerns this evening though, please do call us on 105 and we will do our best to provide support. Sent at 20:23 by Will.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Good evening. Just a quick update to advise that our Customer Champions will be leaving the area at 20:00 and then return tomorrow morning from approx 09:00.

They are still based in the Claremont Road at the moment though so please do visit them if you need any information or support at all. If you are unable to make it to them though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105 or simply 105 and we will be able to get a message to them.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Lee at 15:08 - We believe our engineers have been able to restore power to all 1144 customers in the Wadhurst TN5 6 area by diverting the power around an overhead network fault. We're pleased that we have been able to restore power sooner than we estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be worrying, but please rest assured that our staff work around the clock to keep the lights on. If you have any concerns or questions, give us a call any time.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hello, Lee from the messaging team here. Thanks for your patience today, our field staff and control room engineers are working to get your power back on as quickly as they can.

Our records show that some properties affected by today's power cut may have had other unexpected interruptions recently. If you have, we're sorry for further disruption. All power cuts are fully investigated by our engineers but can sometimes happen multiple times before we can safely find the issue and fix it.

We know that not everyone affected by today's power cut will have had other power cuts recently, but we want you to know that if you have, our customer services team will try to update as many customers as possible about our investigations.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Lee at 14:36 - Thanks for your patience this afternoon.

Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our overhead cabling network. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Decasta at 15:57 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Lee at 17:12 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an underground cable fault that initially interrupted power to 1565 properties. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 2000 - 2100, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: A fault occurred on a piece of our electrical equipment which controls the power to your home.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Decasta at 21:07 - We can now confirm that all power in the Canons Road area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Our engineers have arrived on site in the Canons Road area, the estimated time of 23:30-00:30 hasn't changed.

We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can.

We'll update you with all of our progress, but if you need to speak to us, call our team on 08003163105 any time. If you would like more information about what we think the fault is; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse. Sent by Decasta at 20:54.

Steps.Message:
Engineers have arrived on site in the Colchester area to investigate an overhead cabling fault currently affecting 264 properties. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off.

Using an average timescale for this type of issue we're looking to have you back on between 00:00-01:00, however we must stress that this time may change, depending on what our staff find. We'll continue to update you with all of our progress.

Sent by Decasta at 21:51.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Decasta at 21:31 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an overhead cabling fault that initially interrupted power to 495 properties. However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 264 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 00:00-01:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on?
